metadata.summary: Atomic design system values (colors, spacing, typography) in a three-tier hierarchy: primitive → semantic → component. One JSON source of truth emits per-platform outputs via Style Dictionary.

content.title: Design Tokens
content.summary: Design tokens are the atomic values of a design system — colors, spacing, typography, shadows — stored as structured data with a three-tier hierarchy: primitive (raw values) → semantic (purpose-based aliases) → component (usage-specific). One JSON source of truth emits per-platform outputs (CSS vars, JS constants, iOS Swift, Android XML) via Style Dictionary.

why.p: Without tokens, hardcoded hex values and magic numbers scatter across codebases, making theming impossible and brand changes surgical nightmares. The three-tier model separates "what the value is" from "what it means" from "where it's used," allowing dark mode, white-labeling, and platform consistency without forking components. Agents benefit because they pick values from a constrained vocabulary instead of inventing hex literals.

list.item: Building or extending a design system for web + mobile.
Introducing dark mode or white-label theming.
Bridging Figma to code so design and engineering stay in sync.
Standardizing brand across multiple apps in a monorepo.
Connecting a token pipeline to Tailwind config or platform constants.
Single one-off marketing page — overhead beats payoff.
Apps fully delegating to a UI library (Material, Mantine) with no re-skinning.
Prototype work where designers iterate hourly — token churn outpaces pipeline cost.
Pure server-rendered emails using external template SaaS that owns tokens.

section.name: three-tier-model
section.title: Three-Tier Model

rule.statement: Organize tokens in three tiers: primitive (raw values), semantic (purpose aliases), component (usage-specific). Never skip a tier by referencing primitives from components.
rule.rationale: Skipping tiers couples component styling to raw values. A brand color change then requires touching every component instead of one semantic token.

section.p: Tier definitions: Primitive = the raw value (color-blue-500: #3b82f6). Semantic = the purpose (color-action-primary references color-blue-500). Component = the slot (button-bg-primary references color-action-primary).

rule.statement: Primitive token names describe what the value is (color-blue-500, spacing-4, font-size-base). Semantic token names describe purpose, never a value (color-text-primary, color-bg-secondary). Component token names describe usage (button-padding-horizontal).
rule.rationale: Value-bearing names in semantic tokens (color-blue-primary) break when the brand color changes — the name becomes a lie.

antipattern.description: Using a value-bearing name as a semantic token: color-blue-500-action.
antipattern.reason: When brand blue changes to teal, the token name is wrong and callers must be renamed too.

rule.statement: Store all token tiers as JSON files in a tokens/ directory. Primitive values use string or numeric values. Semantic tokens reference primitives using {category.name} syntax. Generate all platform outputs (CSS, JS, iOS, Android) from this source — never edit generated files.
rule.rationale: A single JSON source of truth means one edit propagates to all platforms automatically.

example.title: Primitive and Semantic Token Structure
example.note: Dark theme is a separate semantic-dark.json that overrides the same keys with different primitive references.

rule.statement: Store tokens in a tokens/ directory: primitive.json, semantic.json, semantic-dark.json, and a components/ subfolder for component-specific tokens. Version the token package separately so consumers adopt at their own pace.
rule.rationale: Separating light and dark themes as distinct files prevents merge conflicts and makes theme-switching logic explicit.

rule.statement: Generate CSS custom properties, JS constants, Tailwind config, and platform-native constants from the token JSON via Style Dictionary. Never hand-write generated files.
rule.rationale: Hand-written outputs drift from the source JSON within weeks. Style Dictionary build as a pre-commit hook keeps outputs always in sync.

rule.statement: Make Style Dictionary build a pre-commit hook. Any token JSON edit that does not regenerate outputs must fail CI.
rule.rationale: Agents edit token JSON and forget to rebuild outputs, shipping inconsistent platform CSS and JS.

example.title: Style Dictionary Build Pipeline

rule.statement: Run WCAG 2.2 AA contrast checks in CI for every semantic text-on-background pairing. Block PRs that drop any pairing below 4.5:1 for normal text or 3:1 for large text.
rule.rationale: Tokens do not enforce contrast — agents compose text-primary on bg-secondary without checking ratios. CI must be the gate.

rule.statement: Require both light and dark semantic tokens for every new semantic key. CI must fail if a key exists in semantic.json but is absent from semantic-dark.json.
rule.rationale: Dark-mode parity gaps produce invisible text or broken UI in dark mode that only shows up in production.

rule.statement: Mark deprecated tokens with "$deprecated": "use color.bg.primary" in JSON. Linters must reject new usages while allowing old code to compile. Provide migration guides for major version bumps.
rule.rationale: Without deprecation markers, dead tokens accumulate silently and no one knows which are safe to remove.

rule.statement: Add a "search before add" rule: before creating a new token, grep the existing JSON for a token with equivalent purpose. Token count above ~200 requires a lifecycle process (deprecate/merge/split).
rule.rationale: Agents create new tokens instead of reusing, causing token explosion where no one can grep the design vocabulary.

antipattern.description: Hardcoding raw hex values or pixel sizes in component code instead of referencing tokens.
antipattern.reason: Hardcoded values are invisible to the token pipeline. Brand changes require grep-and-replace across all components.

antipattern.description: Flat token structure without hierarchy (one level of keys, no primitive/semantic split).
antipattern.reason: Theming becomes impossible: every consumer must be updated individually instead of changing a semantic alias.

antipattern.description: Syncing tokens by hand between Figma and code (copy-pasting hex values).
antipattern.reason: Manual sync rots within days. Figma should be a view onto the JSON, not the source of truth.

antipattern.description: Agents writing bg-[#3b82f6] arbitrary Tailwind values instead of token-backed utilities.
antipattern.reason: Arbitrary values bypass the token pipeline entirely. Lint with eslint-plugin-tailwindcss no-custom-classname.

antipattern.description: Changing a primitive value to fix a single component without reviewing all consumers.
antipattern.reason: Primitives are shared. Changing color-blue-500 may break N other semantic tokens referencing it.

list.item: Three tiers, not two: primitive → semantic → component. Mixing primitive into components hides drift.
Forbid value-bearing names in semantic tokens (color-primary not color-blue-500-primary).
One source of truth — the JSON. Figma is a view onto it, not the truth itself.
Generate, don't write: CSS variables, Tailwind config, and platform constants are build outputs, not source files.
Run contrast checks in CI for every semantic text-on-bg pairing; block PRs that drop below WCAG 2.2 AA.
Visual-regression test the theme switch path; tokens that look right alone often clash in composition.
Version the token package separately (@org/tokens@x.y.z) so consumers can adopt at their own pace; semver carefully.
Mark deprecations in JSON with "$deprecated": "use color.bg.primary"; lints can refuse new usages while letting old code compile.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.title: Agentic Workflow
section.p: A token agent maintains the canonical JSON, runs Style Dictionary to emit per-platform outputs (CSS vars, JS const, Tailwind config, iOS/Android), and runs a contrast-check pass on every semantic pairing. A second agent watches Figma via the Tokens Studio plugin or Variables API and proposes a PR diff for the JSON. A reviewer agent checks naming rules and flags dead tokens.

list.item: faion-sdd-executor-agent — design + implement + verify with visual regression as quality gate.
A custom token-linter (haiku) — enforces naming rules and detects unused tokens via repo grep.
A custom contrast-auditor (sonnet) — reads semantic pairings (text-primary on bg-primary) and runs WCAG 2.2 contrast math.
A custom figma-syncer (sonnet) — pulls Figma Variables → emits JSON diff PR.

section.p: Prompt pattern: Audit tokens/ in this repo: list primitive vs semantic vs component tiers; find primitives referenced 0 times (dead tokens); find semantic pairings whose contrast < 4.5:1 for body text; propose rename PR: any name containing a value (e.g. blue-500 used as semantic). Output: report.md + suggested diff. Do not auto-apply.

list.item: Agents create new tokens instead of reusing — require a "search before add" rule with rg against the JSON.
LLMs invent semantic names that bake in values (color-blue-action) — token-linter must reject.
Agents change a primitive value to "fix" a single component, breaking N other consumers — require ownership review per primitive.
Agents skip dark-mode entries, leaving theme parity gaps; CI must require both light and dark for every semantic key.
Tailwind: agents bypass tokens by writing bg-[#3b82f6] arbitrary values — lint with eslint-plugin-tailwindcss no-custom-classname.
Human-in-loop checkpoint: any change to brand-tier primitives (logo color, brand blue) must require explicit design owner approval.
Agents forget to regenerate platform outputs on token edits — make Style Dictionary build a pre-commit hook.
style-dictionary — Compile token JSON to per-platform outputs. npm i -D style-dictionary · https://styledictionary.com
token-transformer — Tokens Studio → Style Dictionary bridge. npm i -D token-transformer
terrazzo (formerly Cobalt) — Modern token compiler with W3C DTCG support. https://terrazzo.app
theo — Salesforce's token compiler (legacy but stable). https://github.com/salesforce-ux/theo
figma-tokens / Tokens Studio plugin — Authoring tokens inside Figma. https://tokens.studio
axe-core / pa11y — A11y/contrast checks against rendered components. https://github.com/dequelabs/axe-core
chromatic / loki / playwright — Visual regression for theme switches. https://www.chromatic.com
tailwindcss — Consume token JSON via tailwind.config. https://tailwindcss.com
Tokens Studio (Figma plugin) — SaaS, agent-friendly. Has REST/Sync API; agents can read JSON exports.
Specify — SaaS, agent-friendly. Token sync platform with API.
Supernova — SaaS, agent-friendly. Design system platform; CLI for export.
Knapsack — SaaS, partial agent support. Design system docs; less agent-driven.
Storybook + addon-themes — OSS, agent-friendly. Theme-switch playground + docs.
Chromatic — SaaS, agent-friendly. CI gate for theme regressions.
GitHub Actions — SaaS, agent-friendly. Run Style Dictionary on every PR; commit generated outputs.

template.purpose: Style Dictionary pipeline emitting CSS vars, JS ES6, and iOS Swift from token JSON
Primitive token JSON structure with color, spacing, typography, radius, and shadow scales
Semantic token JSON with light-theme aliases and dark-theme extension pattern
